question: Define a class Distance that will have Feet and Inches as its private members. Now make operator overloading functions ++, --, = and an over loaded function – (minus). The description of these function is as follow ++ will add 1 in feet and inches of the distance object, -- will subtract 1 from feet and inches of that object, = will give value of one object to another and – will simple negate the values that object. You have to define getters and setter as well.

test_cases.expected:
Original d1: 5 feet 8 inches
After ++d1: 6 feet 9 inches
After --d1: 5 feet 8 inches
d2 = d1: 5 feet 8 inches
d3 = -d1: -5 feet -8 inches
d4 before decrement: 0 feet 0 inches
d4 after decrement: 0 feet 0 inches

driver: int main() { Distance d1(5, 8); Distance d2; cout << "Original d1: "; d1.display(); ++d1; cout << "After ++d1: "; d1.display(); --d1; cout << "After --d1: "; d1.display(); d2 = d1; cout << "d2 = d1: "; d2.display(); Distance d3 = -d1; cout << "d3 = -d1: "; d3.display(); Distance d4(0, 0); cout << "d4 before decrement: "; d4.display(); --d4; cout << "d4 after decrement: "; d4.display(); return 0; }
